RiftBeacon.name: Rift Beacon
RiftBeacon.pickup:
Survive a deadly hit by tearing open a

rift.
RiftBeacon.description:
Dropping below 25% health grants a barrier for 25% of maximum health and opens a

rift around you for 8 seconds. The rift periodically knocks enemies back within 7 meters, and each kill extends it by 1 second. After triggering, the beacon breaks and repairs at the start of each stage.
RiftBeacon.destination:
Quarantine Safe 17,
Engineering Archive,
UES Contact Light
RiftBeacon.story:
The recording inside the beacon, discovered before the cadets examined it, contained their own voices. Its timestamp was 17 minutes ahead of the present.

At first they thought it was a technicians' joke, but the recording continued:

— Fine, I'll touch it.
— Wait. There is something else here.
— It can hear us.

The last words were spoken by a third voice.

The cadets handed the beacon to the senior engineer. He checked the serial number and established that the device had been aboard the UES since the ship was built and had never been removed.

The engineer ordered the find destroyed.

Seventeen minutes later, the beacon began transmitting a new recording.

RiftBeaconDeactivate.name: Broken Rift Beacon
RiftBeaconDeactivate.pickup: Repairs at the start of the next stage.
RiftBeaconDeactivate.description:
Repairs into

Rift Beacon at the start of the next stage.

IngrownIdol.name: Ingrown Idol
IngrownIdol.pickup: Feeds on <r>kills</c> and, when sated, looses a <r>beast</c> to hunt for you.
IngrownIdol.description: Each <r>kill</c> feeds the idol: a normal enemy gives <y>1 offering</c>, an elite gives <y>2</c>, and a boss gives <y>3</c>. Every <b>30 offerings</c>, the idol summons a <r>powerful beast</c> that hunts enemies for <b>45 seconds</c> and deals <r>200% damage</c> <c_stack>(+50% per stack)</c>. After <b>20 minutes</c>, the value of offerings is halved (<y>0.5</c> / <y>1</c> / <y>2</c>).
IngrownIdol.story:
In the northern forests, they say that some gods are not sent down from the sky or raised from the earth — they are fed.

It begins with a figurine of a dead root, carried for luck on the hunt. Over time it grows heavier and becomes covered in cracks, as though growing inward.

Old priests claimed that such an idol does not depict the beast; it builds a path for it.

When the path is ready, the beast itself comes out of the forest: lean, with a deer's skull instead of a face, covered in moss. It makes no sound — it simply follows whoever has carried the idol for long enough.

And the forest parts before it.

ContagionBarb.name: Contagion Barb
ContagionBarb.description:
8% (+8% per stack) chance on hit to infect an enemy with

disease, dealing 90% TOTAL damage over 4 seconds. Killing an infected enemy transfers the disease to 1 (+1 per stack) nearest enemy.
ContagionBarb.story:
When the colony was first discovered, it was already dying.

The workers continued carrying food into empty cells. Guards protected entrances beyond which nothing had remained for a long time. In the center of the hive lay the queen — enormous, motionless, and covered in metal growths.

Tubes had been implanted in her abdomen. Control needles, at the bases of her wings. Even her stinger had been replaced with an artificial one.

Dr. Vale ordered everyone to keep away.

— She is not sick, she said. — They are making her into a disease.

Command demanded that the specimen be delivered intact.

We were able to extract only one barb.

On the way back, Vale destroyed all records of the expedition and threw the container into the airlock.

The container returned aboard with the next cargo.

CharlatansDie.name: Charlatan's Die
CharlatansDie.pickup: Roll the die to <b>cheat a chest</c>.
CharlatansDie.description: The <b>first chest</c> each stage has a <b>35%</c> <c_stack>(+20% per stack)</c> chance to grant an additional <g>higher-rarity</c> item. No more than <b>3 additional items</c> can drop.
CharlatansDie.destination:
Treasure Vault of Six,
Hall of Unfulfilled Wishes,
[REDACTED]
CharlatansDie.story:
Six entered the treasure vault.

The first asked for gold.
The second, for weapons.
The third, for immortality.
The fourth, for love.
The fifth, for knowledge.

The sixth asked for nothing. He rolled the die and told the others to name the number that came up. Each saw a different one.

When the vault doors opened, only the sixth came out. He carried other people's gold, other people's weapons, and five scrolls with names crossed out.

They asked him what the die had shown.

The charlatan smiled.

— What was needed.

A one was carved on all six faces.

StandingRequisition.name: Standing Requisition
StandingRequisition.pickup: Each stage delivers a <b>choice</c> of items.
StandingRequisition.description: At the start of each stage, a <b>delivery crate</c> appears with a <b>choice of 2</c> items (<b>79%</c>/<g>20%</c>/<r>1%</c>). Take one item for free and the other for money. More items increase the <b>rarity</c> odds.
StandingRequisition.destination:
Supply Department,
Distribution Queue,
UES Contact Light
StandingRequisition.story:
//--UES CONTACT LIGHT AUTOMATIC NOTIFICATION --//

Recipient: {crewMemberName}
Requisition status: STANDING
Category: Supply

Your application for regular receipt of support equipment has been approved.

To optimize cargo space, the supply system will offer two available items from the approved list. After one item is selected, the other will be returned to the distribution queue, written off as surplus, or redirected to another employee with an equivalent access level.

Please note: a standing requisition does not guarantee that you will receive the requested equipment. A standing requisition guarantees only the standing processing of the requisition.

UES thanks you for your understanding.

Current delivery point: UES Contact Light
Next delivery point: ERROR
Next delivery point: ERROR
Next delivery point: ERROR

LootFever.name: Loot Fever
LootFever.description: Picking up an <b>item</c> increases all main stats for <b>20 seconds</c> <c_stack>(+20 seconds per stack)</c>. The bonus depends on the <y>rarity of the find</c> and stacks up to <y>15%</c> <c_stack>(+15% per stack)</c>.
LootFever.destination:
Auction Dock,
Krez Station,
Outer Colonies
LootFever.story:
After "Black Friday" was banned in 2034, major retail chains abandoned public sales.

This did not solve the problem.

Sales moved to closed clubs, then to corporate auctions, and later to colonial markets, where local laws were virtually absent.

The most famous was the incident at Krez Station: after the sale of cargo from a missing research vessel was announced, more than three thousand people gathered in the docks. When the containers were opened, they held faulty geological scanners, cheap prosthetics, and several crates of expired rations.

Eighty-two people died for the right to reach them first.

The survivors later claimed that, at that moment, the cargo's contents had seemed priceless to them.

— "History of Extraterrestrial Trade," Volume IV

MagicMissile.name: Magic Missile
MagicMissile.pickup:
Your primary skill fires a

magic missile. Recharges over time.
MagicMissile.description:
Activating your primary skill creates a

magic missile that deals 120% (+60% per stack) base damage and pierces up to 4 enemies. Holds up to 4 (+2 per stack) missiles. Each missile recharges in 2.5 seconds.
MagicMissile.destination:
Leisure Deck 4,
Quartermaster Stores,
UES DnD Adventure
MagicMissile.story:
Excerpt from a field journal. Found in a tabletop-game enthusiast's footlocker, between a bag of dice and a worn rulebook:

"...at first I thought it was merely an arrow fragment.

But as soon as a creature appeared nearby, the shard lifted into the air by itself. Fine violet sparks flared around it, and carved words appeared on the shaft: ‘target visible.’"

BountyContract.name: Bounty Contract
BountyContract.description: Instantly <r>kills an elite monster</c> below <y>12%</c> <c_stack>(+12% per stack)</c> health. Every <r>elite monster kill</c> grants <y>$15</c> <c_stack>(+$15 per stack)</c>. <y>Gold increases over time</c>.
BountyContract.destination:
Contracts Desk,
Reclamation Office,
Primas V
BountyContract.story:
Notice issued by the Provisional Government of Primas V:

The guillotine remains intended for enemies of the people whose deaths must be public.

For those who fled, concealed their status, bought silence, or hid behind foreign documents, official contracts may now be concluded. Payment will be made after confirmation that the specified target has been eliminated.

Use red ink only. Do not replace it with black ink. Do not cross out a name once it has been written.

If the reward amount exceeds the stated sum, report the discrepancy to the Land Reclamation Office.

Golemshot.name: Golemshot
Golemshot.description: Activating any <b>interactable</c> deploys a <y>golem turret</c> for <b>30s</c> that fires at the nearest enemy within <b>~250</c> every <b>0.75s</c> for <y>100% damage</c> <c_stack>(+50% per stack)</c>. Up to <b>1</c> <c_stack>(+1 per stack)</c> golem at once.

BombardierSwarm.name: Bombardier Swarm
BombardierSwarm.pickup: Summons a <y>bombardier drone</c> that drops <r>dynamite</c> on enemies.
BombardierSwarm.description: Summons a bombardier drone. Every <b>10 seconds</c>, it drops dynamite dealing <y>250%</c> <c_stack>(+125% per stack)</c> <y>base damage</c> in a <b>150</c> radius and <b>stunning</c> enemies for <b>1 second</c>. Your nearby drones also drop dynamite.

RustMite.name: Rust Mite
RustMite.description: On hit, has a <y>100%</c> chance to apply <r>Rust</c>. Each stack of <r>Rust</c> increases damage the target takes from <b>any source</c> by <r>+4%</c> <c_stack>(+4% per stack)</c> <r>permanently</c>.
RustMite.destination:
Cargo Container 7-C,
Maintenance Log,
UES Contact Light
RustMite.story:
//--UES CONTACT LIGHT MAINTENANCE-LOG FRAGMENT --//

Local damage to the protective coating on cargo container 7-C detected.

Primary cause: corrosion.
Secondary cause: movement of corrosion.
Tertiary cause: corrosion has legs.

Recommendation: isolate the container.

Addendum: container isolation failed.
Addendum: isolation from the container also failed.
Addendum: do not touch the orange dust.

MosquitoNet.name: Mosquito Net
MosquitoNet.pickup: <b>Shoot down</c> projectiles and periodically <b>block a hit</c>.
MosquitoNet.description: Every <b>0.5 seconds</c>, shoots down <b>2</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c> <r>enemy projectiles</c> within <b>5m</c> (recharge <b>scales with attack speed</c>). Every <b>7 seconds</c> <c_stack>(-10% per stack)</c>, fully <b>blocks</c> one incoming hit if it deals more than <b>10%</c> of current health.
MosquitoNet.story:
Field journal, entry damaged:

At first, we laughed at the name.

"Mosquito net." Very funny. Especially on a planet where the smallest "mosquito" spits plasma the size of a human head.

Then the device clicked.

Once — and the fireball turned to ash.

Then again.

And again.

No one laughed anymore.

We just listened to the clicks and hoped the next one would sound too.

OrbitalLens.name: Orbital Lens
OrbitalLens.pickup: Chance to call a <r>meteor</c> on hit.
OrbitalLens.description: <y>3%</c> <c_stack>(+2% per stack)</c> chance on hit to call a <r>meteor</c> for <y>800%</c> <c_stack>(+250% per stack)</c> base damage. The more <y>damage you deal</c>, the <y>higher the chance and meteor damage</c> (decays over time).
OrbitalLens.story:
Safe-use instructions:

Do not point the lens at allies.
Do not point the lens at vehicles.
Do not point the lens at residential modules, medical tents, command posts, or any objects you would prefer to keep.
Do not point the lens upward.
Do not point the lens downward.
Do not cover the lens with your hand after targeting begins.

If you see a blinking dot in the center of the glass, place the device on the ground and quickly leave the marked area.

If the dot stops blinking, quickly will no longer help.

LeackedCapacitor.name: Leaking Capacitor
LeackedCapacitor.pickup: Periodic <b>wave</c> that <y>energizes allies</c> and empowers drones.
LeackedCapacitor.description: All drones gain <y>+15%</c> <c_stack>(+15% per stack)</c> attack speed. Every <b>15 seconds</c> <c_stack>(-20% per stack)</c>, releases a wave that <y>energizes allies</c> for <b>7 seconds</c>. Energizing grants <y>+30% attack and movement speed</c>.
LeackedCapacitor.destination:
Cargo Bay 3-B,
Power-Cell Depot,
UES Contact Light
LeackedCapacitor.story:
//--MAINTENANCE LOG: CARGO BAY 3-B --//

Reason for equipment failure: short circuit.

Source of short circuit: portable power cell.

Power-cell condition: discharged.

Second check: discharged.

Third check: discharged.

Technician's note: if the cell is discharged, why does it keep shorting everything around it?

Addendum: cease checking.

Addendum: the technician's tool evaporated.

RavenousVine.name: Ravenous Vine
RavenousVine.pickup: Nearby enemies <g>feed the vine</c>; when ripe, it <r>explodes</c> and <g>heals</c>.
RavenousVine.description: Enemies within <b>2 meters</c> <c_stack>(+1 meter per stack)</c> <g>feed the vine</c>. Every <b>0.5 seconds</c>, it deals <r>70% base damage</c> <c_stack>(+30% per stack)</c> to all nearby enemies. The more enemies are nearby, the faster the vine ripens. When ripe, it explodes for <r>400% base damage</c> <c_stack>(+200% per stack)</c> and heals for <g>12% maximum health</c> <c_stack>(+6% per stack)</c>.
RavenousVine.story:
Your plant no longer needs expensive branded soil!

The new self-sustaining root system independently finds organic nutrients throughout the workspace.

Simply place the pot in a busy area and enjoy improved morale, cleaner air, and a noticeable drop in sick-leave requests.

CascadeFailure.name: Cascade Failure
CascadeFailure.description: <r>Infect</c> up to <b>4</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c> enemies. <y>50%</c> of damage you deal to infected goes into a <b>shared buffer</c>. Every <b>3 seconds</c>, all infected take <y>100%</c> of the damage in the <b>buffer</c>.
CascadeFailure.destination:
Synchronization Test Stand,
Autonomous Systems Laboratory,
Jovian Complex
CascadeFailure.story:
"We have another problem with damage synchronization."

"Drones?"

"Not only. Turrets, sensors, emergency beacons. One module fails, and the rest continue to consider it operational."

"Add a status check."

"We did."

"And?"

"Now they know about the failure."

"Excellent."

"No. Now they repeat it."

ArmySurplus.name: Army Surplus
ArmySurplus.pickup: Gain a <b>combat drone</c> and reinforce your drones.
ArmySurplus.description: Deploys a <b>combat drone</c>. Drones gain <y>60%</c> <c_stack>(+60% per stack)</c> damage. <g>25%</c> of the player's additional healing is passed to <b>drones</c>.
ArmySurplus.destination:
Decommissioned Property Warehouse,
Cargo Bay,
UES Contact Light

CashbackChip.name: Cashback Chip
CashbackChip.description:
Refunds <y>10%</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> of the <y>gold</c> spent on <b>any purchase</c>. In addition, the <y>first chest</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> each stage is <y>free</c>.
<c_stack>Each Cashback Chip increases the chance to open a chest for free by 10%. The number of free chests is unlimited.</c>
CashbackChip.story:
//--AUTOMATIC TRANSCRIPT FROM UES CONTACT LIGHT --//

"Check again. Why does the cargo terminal return money after every authorized purchase?"

"It is not a refund, sir. It is cashback."

"I know what cashback is. I am asking why it is applied to the crew's emergency expenses."

"Because emergency expenses are considered priority operating costs."

"And who pays them?"

"First, the crew."

"And then?"

"Then the system returns them a percentage."

"From which budget?"

"From the personnel-retention budget."

"We have a personnel-retention budget?"

"After the latest mortality report — yes."

"That does not solve the problem. It merely shifts expenses from one department to another."

"No, sir. If the chip is installed correctly, it shifts them fast enough for the problem to appear as profit."

//--END OF TRANSCRIPT --//

SoulJar.name: Soul Jar
SoulJar.pickup: Summon a <r>shadow assassin</c> that blinks between enemies.
SoulJar.description: On use, summons a <r>shade</c> for <b>13 seconds</c> that blinks to the nearest enemy and deals <r>133% damage</c>.
SoulJar.story:
APHELIAN VESSEL NO. 41 REPORT

The object is made of fired clay and decorated with ornaments from the late period of desert settlements. Its inner surface is coated with an unknown organic substance that restores its volume after removal.

After the object was placed near animal remains, sounds of movement began coming from inside.

On the fourth day, staff observed a silhouette near the urn that was not recorded by cameras.

On the fifth day, the silhouette took the form of the deceased leader of the previous expedition and demanded to be returned home.

TacticalVisor.name: Tactical Visor
TacticalVisor.description: On use, gain <y>+100% Critical Chance</c> for <b>8 seconds</c>.
TacticalVisor.destination:
Optics Bay,
Marksman Range 3,
UES Contact Light
TacticalVisor.story:
TACTICAL VISOR TV-4

Automatic threat recognition!
Advanced combat analytics!
Personnel color coding:

Red — enemy.
Blue — ally.
Yellow — losses acceptable.

If the visor conflicts with the commander's order, follow the visor's instructions. The company reminds you: the visor processes more information and has no privileges.

WARNING: prolonged use may cause migraines, insomnia, and the sensation of an outside gaze. These symptoms indicate incorrect calibration.

Hunger.name: Hunger
Hunger.pickup:
Open a

black hole that pulls enemies in.
Hunger.description:
On use, launch a

black hole that drifts forward and pulls enemies toward its center for 10 seconds. Deals no damage; bosses are immune.
Hunger.story:
At first, the Maw lived between the stars, feeding on light and ships.

But that was not enough.

So it shrank and hid in a vessel that people called a weapon.

The Maw did not touch giants. It knew that one day it would become even larger.

Annihilator.name: Annihilator
Annihilator.description: On use, launches a slow <b>charge</c> that strikes nearby enemies with lightning, dealing up to <r>222% damage per second</c>. On contact it <r>detonates</c> in a huge explosion with a <b>22-meter</c> radius, dealing <r>2222% damage</c>.
Annihilator.destination:
Reactor Ridge,
Weapons Test Annex,
Military Complex "Thunder"
Annihilator.story:
PROJECT NO. 22: FINAL REPORT

After the loss of twenty-one preon batteries, it was decided to create a device that would not need to be recovered after use.

Customer requirements included increased durability, an autonomous guidance system, and enough power to destroy a fortified target.

All three requirements were met.

The last one, with substantial excess.

During the first test, the device left its rail, ignored its designated target, and headed for the observation complex.

On the way, it struck twenty-two moving targets in sequence, including two vehicles, an emergency drone, and an engineer who had left the safe zone.

Contact with the exterior wall caused detonation.

Of the complex's twenty-two underground levels, one remained.

Project closed.

FacetedJade.name: Faceted Jade
FacetedJade.description: <g>Increases armor</c> by <g>45</c> <c_stack>(+45 per stack)</c> while you are out of danger. After taking damage, the bonus remains for <b>3 seconds</c>.
FacetedJade.story:
Excerpt from the folk tale "Pure as Jade":

"...and when the battle had passed, the carver gathered the shards that had fallen from the stone hide of the great elephant. A pale green light still glowed in each of them, though their power was no longer that of the heavenly stone.

The people wore these facets close to their hearts, believing that jade would not let fear find them first. They said arrows slipped past, blades lost their true path, and evil intent grew dull — as long as the bearer's soul remained calm.

But once blood was spilled, the light within the shard fell silent..."

ElephantGun.name: Elephant Gun
ElephantGun.description: Deal <y>+15%</c> <c_stack>(+15% per stack)</c> more damage and gain <y>+10% Critical Chance</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> against <r>bosses</c>.
ElephantGun.destination:
Big-Game Reserve,
Trophy Hold,
Savannah Belt, Earth
ElephantGun.story:
A rifle too heavy for any sane shoulder, bored wide enough to swallow a fist. It was never meant for small things - fire it at a scurrying target and you'll only deafen yourself. But aim it at something vast, something that thinks its size makes it safe, and the round goes in like a verdict.

The big-game hunter who carried it kept one rule in his log: «Never waste it on what you could simply outrun. Save it for what you cannot.»

BloodyDividends.name: Bloody Dividends
BloodyDividends.description: On taking <r>damage</c> from an enemy, gain <y>$6</c> <c_stack>(+$6 per stack)</c>. If the hit removes more than <b>25%</c> of your maximum health, gain twice as much. <y>Increases over time</c>.
BloodyDividends.story:
The last Lemurian fell into the dust, and for a moment the battlefield was quiet.

Johnson looked around, but no one from his team got back up. Only smoke, blood, and the distant howls of creatures beyond the rocks.

With difficulty, he rose, readjusted his weapon, and smiled through the pain.

— I hope they pay overtime too.

CheatDeath.name: Cheat Death
CheatDeath.description: <b>10%</c> <c_stack>(+5% per stack)</c> of <r>damage taken</c> is spread over <b>5 seconds</c> as <r>bleed damage</c>.
CheatDeath.story:
Gunshot wound to the chest. On admission there were no signs of blood loss, but massive internal bleeding began after 5 seconds.

A second intervention before the fifth second had elapsed lessened the symptoms.

After the deck was removed, all delayed damage manifested. Patient No. 17 died.

The cards were deemed suitable for further study.

Concentration.name: Concentration
Concentration.pickup: Being <g>out of danger</c> empowers your <y>next hit</c>.
Concentration.description: After <r>taking no damage for 3 seconds</c>, your <y>next attack</c> hits <y>60%</c> <c_stack>(+60% per stack)</c> harder. Consumed on hit.
Concentration.story:
I bought a little ring from your antiques shop at a discount. The one with the strange eye-shaped crystal. I... I want to return it.

At first everything was fine. It just sat on my finger like an ordinary trinket. Then it started staring. Not merely looking — watching. Waiting.

I panicked and ran without looking where I was going. At the corner I accidentally crashed into some guy, and he was thrown to the other end of the street as if I had hit him with the whole body of a cargo loader.

Take this cursed thing back. Just don't let it look at me again.

Tankbuster.name: Tankbuster
Tankbuster.description: Every <b>5 seconds</c>, your next hit deals an additional <y>100%</c> <c_stack>(+70% per stack)</c> of base damage and <y>4%</c> <c_stack>(+2% per stack)</c> of the enemy's <r>maximum health</c>.
Tankbuster.destination:
Cursed Apple,
New New York,
Dead Castle
Tankbuster.story:
A homemade anti-tank bomb from the civil-war incident with occultists in New York. Its destructive potential is incredibly low against small targets and infantry, yet against heavy machinery its effectiveness exceeds every allowable limit.

How is unclear.

Sergeant, I swear to you — I have no idea how it works. Thirteen years in military engineering, and not a single thought as to what makes this damned contraption function that way. When we opened it, a green puff of smoke came out of the casing, but inside there was nothing except crude wiring, scorch marks, and occult symbols on the shell itself.

Hell knows what those spiritualists used to put it together. But one thing is clear — they certainly know what to use for magic.

BountyTag.name: Bounty
BountyTag.description: Earn <y>$2</c> <c_stack>(+$2 per stack)</c> per <r>kill</c>, plus <y>$1</c> for each consecutive kill without taking damage, up to <y>+$10</c>. Taking damage <r>resets the streak</c>. The reward <y>increases over time</c>.
BountyTag.destination:
Contracts Desk,
Bounty Office,
Edge Frontiers
BountyTag.story:
The front of the tag bore a laughing horned man. On the reverse were the target's name and an amount.

After a kill, the name vanished and the amount grew.

Sometimes the next target appeared.

Sometimes the target was very close.

Once, the owner's own name appeared on the reverse.

The bounty was a record amount.

OneArmyMan.name: One Army Man
OneArmyMan.pickup: Against <r>legions</c>, we will send you alone. <r>Rip and tear</c> until they are exhausted!
OneArmyMan.description: On use, <b>BFG</c> begins playing for <b>30 seconds</c>. For the first <b>22 seconds</c>, increases <y>attack speed</c> and <y>damage</c> by <y>50%</c>, and each <r>kill</c> restores <g>1.5% of maximum health</c>. For the duration, the number of enemies on the map <r>increases significantly</c>. Summoned enemies <r>grant no experience</c> and yield <y>less gold</c>.
OneArmyMan.story:
Armies were raised against the spawn of the depths. Walls were built against unholy legions. Gods were invoked for victory.

The armies were torn apart. The walls fell. The gods did not answer.

Then one warrior was sent through the gate.

Years passed, but the legions never came back out. Instead, they rushed deeper, shoving and crushing one another in a desperate attempt to hide from the one they had once considered their victim.

HeavyLungs.name: Heavy Lungs
HeavyLungs.description: Increases <g>drone maximum health</c> and <g>regeneration</c> by <g>15%</c> <c_stack>(+15% per stack)</c>.
HeavyLungs.destination:
Pulmonary Complex,
Life-Support Bay,
UES PRIME
HeavyLungs.story:
Heavy breathing bellows, cast from lead, work almost silently. Engineers fitted them to old drones to improve endurance and resistance to overloads.

We tested them on our own drones: under fire they held out longer, returned to service faster, and shut down far less often after critical overloads.

The report called them an "auxiliary life-support circuit for autonomous machines." On the lower decks they put it more simply: even a tin can has an easier time surviving if it has something to breathe with.

LaughingFungus.name: Laughing Fungus
LaughingFungus.description: After <b>standing still for 1 second</c>, you create a zone that every second <g>heals</c> all <g>allies</c> within <b>3 meters</c> <c_stack>(+1.5m per stack)</c> for <g>6%</c> <c_stack>(+3% per stack)</c> of <g>your maximum health</c>.
LaughingFungus.story:
The Laughing Fungus does not hide underground like its more common relative.

There is no need.

Most creatures that disturb the colony soon cease their attacks. First they make short, sharp sounds, then lose their balance, then fall beside the mycelium, still shaking with laughter.

Spores germinate better in moist soil.

Tears will do.

AssemblyBlueprint.name: Assembly Blueprint
AssemblyBlueprint.pickup: Chance to <b>duplicate purchased drones</c> and <g>rebuild dead ones</c>.
AssemblyBlueprint.description: <b>20%</c> <c_stack>(+20% per stack)</c> chance to <b>duplicate a drone on purchase</c>. <g>10%</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> chance to <g>rebuild a drone when it dies</c>, returning it with <g>50% health</c>.
AssemblyBlueprint.destination:
Assembly Line 7,
Assembly Dock,
Space Factory
AssemblyBlueprint.story:
The confiscated assembly blueprint does not conform to any known UES standard.

The composition of its alloys, the arrangement of its components, and even the dimensions of its parts change depending on the equipment near the document.

Corrections appear directly on the paper, without traces of ink, printing, or mechanical intervention.

GoldenSneakers.name: Golden Sneakers
GoldenSneakers.pickup: Grants <b>movement speed</c> based on your current <y>gold</c>.
GoldenSneakers.description: Grants up to <b>+50% movement speed</c> based on your current <y>gold</c>. Reaches the cap at <y>700 gold</c> <c_stack>(-50% gold per stack)</c>. <y>Scales over time</c>.
GoldenSneakers.story:
Gold is rarely chosen for convenience.

It is too heavy for shoes, too soft for soles, and too conspicuous for anyone trying to run away.

Yet this pair of sneakers weighs almost nothing while its owner has something jingling in their pockets. The more gold they carry, the lighter each step becomes — as though the metal itself pulls their feet forward, eager to be somewhere else.

Spend the last of it, and the shoes grow heavy again. Not like gold. Like regret.

UnfocusPrisma.name: Defocused Prism
UnfocusPrisma.description: Increases <y>damage</c> to enemies <b>more than 8 meters</c> away by <y>40%</c> <c_stack>(+40% per stack)</c>.
UnfocusPrisma.destination:
Gravenkamp,
5th Houston Street,
Prism Tower,
Earth
UnfocusPrisma.story:
Order: Divergent prism [0.1 kg, 40 cm x 40 cm x 40 cm]

After the incident with that condensed-matter crystal, I decided to clarify in advance: no, this thing is not heavy. It simply makes everything around it feel more... distant. Especially what is directly in front of you.

ReserveCapacitor.name: Reserve Capacitor
ReserveCapacitor.description: Holds an <b>additional equipment charge</c> <c_stack>(+1 per stack)</c>. Each reserve charge recharges over <y>2x</c> the equipment's cooldown.
ReserveCapacitor.destination:
Reserve Armory,
Power-Cell Depot,
New Earth
ReserveCapacitor.story:
"Fuel was never the final problem.

However, after the transition to compact fuel cells, malfunctions began in related equipment. Research facilities, emergency anchors, shield generators, surgical drones, and coffee makers stopped working.

Everything that required energy pulses proved vulnerable. If the crew showed patience, a properly installed module could be used again after the main cycle was complete."

— A Brief History of Interplanetary Achievements, Volume 3

SeaFossil.name: Sea Fossil
SeaFossil.description: Using equipment <g>Restores 120%</c> <c_stack>(+50% per stack)</c> <g>health</c> from cooldown time.
SeaFossil.story:
We found it at the edge of the shore, where sand no longer holds the step and the water drags you down. On the outside-an ordinary shell.

Put it to your ear and you won't hear the sea. Instead-a measured <b>hammer's beat</c>, as hollow as in the temple beneath the throne hall. Sometimes, slow, heavy steps too, as if someone still walks in circles, unaware all are gone. I recalled tales of the <r>'King without a kingdom'</c> and shuddered: can even stone and shell carry his echoes?

It grants <g>a drop of strength</c>, but seems to demand part of the will in return. The hand reaches for tools on its own, as in that cursed 'gesture', when the tool controls you more than you control it.

We didn't take it on the march. We placed it in a chest marked 'pseudo-strength'. Such things are easier left behind. Too often what looks like support is only a reminder that there are forces not to be envied-and they do not forgive curiosity.

Community.name: Community
Community.description: For each <b>item</c>, you gain <b>1%</c> <c_stack>(+1% per stack)</c> to all stats.
Community.destination:
Litovigla Cluster,
Multicut Mine,
Edge Frontiers
Community.story:
They say every world has its king.
We had a <r>TYRANT</c>, they had a <r>BULWARK</c>. Both held swords wider than a man, both believed they protected the weak from the strong.

But a throne is no fortress. A crown cannot save you from the one who comes nameless and merciless. He comes to every world, tears everyone from their pedestal, and swords are equally dull before the one called hero.

We applaud when he topples the throne, and fall silent when he destroys the world.

Maybe heroes are not protectors, but simply <y>executioners</c> who recognize neither <r>thrones</c> nor <g>gods</c>, trading a crown for the next victim. And if so — should we envy those called <g>kings</c>?

GlassMagnifier.name: Glass Magnifier
GlassMagnifier.description: Increases <y>drone base damage</c> by <y>4</c> <c_stack>(+3 per stack)</c>.
GlassMagnifier.story:
An old magnifier. The brass has darkened, but the glass is clean - as if polished only yesterday.

Look through it and you don't see farther, or larger. You see where it will break. The hairline crack beneath a rivet, the tired grain in a steel beam, the very spot where a thing is already ready to split at a touch. One of our men, studying a lathe through it, muttered: "It doesn't magnify. It passes sentence."

Later he stopped looking at machines. His last notes are about people: whose armor ran thin, whose hand wavered, whose - as he put it - "smile is hanging by a single thread." We put the magnifier away for good - after we found it aimed at a mirror, with him beside it.

ThinWings.name: Thin Wings
ThinWings.pickup: Reloads a <y>skill</c> and increases <b>movement speed</c> when interacting with an object.
ThinWings.description: When interacting with an object, increases <y>movement speed</c> by <b>15%</c><c_stack> (+8% per stack)</c>, and <b>reduces cooldown</c> of a random skill by <b>10%</c> for <b>10 seconds</c>.
ThinWings.story:
Large dragonfly wings, frozen in ice. Fragile to the eye, harder than bone to the touch, and plainly not made of chitin.

Fasten them to your back, and your hands reach for any mechanism on their own: for a lever, a latch, a humming box. They reach before you've decided to. By the end of the second week the testers no longer said "I opened it." They said "it opened."

MassacreCurse.name: Massacre Curse
MassacreCurse.description: Killing an <y>enemy</c> grants <y>1 stack</c><r> of 'Massacre'</c> for <y>2.5</c> seconds <c_stack>(+2.5 seconds)</c>. Increases <y>damage</c> and <g>armor</c> by <y>3%</c>.
MassacreCurse.story:
Another find from the same site. A small skull with a spine attached. Its color is strange: the bone seems to have absorbed <r>blood</c>. We even tried to wash it clean, but the color remained. At times, a white glow flickers in the sockets.

The soldiers say the artifact responds to killing. The more corpses around, the heavier it feels in hand, yet the stronger its bearer becomes. Sounds like nonsense, but we counted far too many bones near its previous owner.

MagmaFragment.name: Magma Fragment
MagmaFragment.description: Your <y>drones</c> have a <y>10%</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> <y>chance</c> to <r>ignite</c> enemies, dealing <y>30% TOTAL damage</c> <b>once per second for 5 seconds</c>. Maximum <b>2 burns</c> <c_stack>(+1 per stack)</c> active at once.
MagmaFragment.story:
Found it in the ashes of the east wing, still warm three days after the fire. Doesn't cool. Doesn't go out. You can hold it for about a second before it bites.

The investigators ruled the blaze an accident. They didn't find the letters, or what was left of them. Good.

This time, she definitely won't find out.

IceMixture.name: Ice Mixture
IceMixture.description: <y>7%</c> <c_stack>(+5% per stack)</c> <y>chance</c> to <b>slow movement speed</c> and <y>attack speed</c> of enemies by 30% for <b>3 seconds</c>.
IceMixture.story:
The first sample from the cryo program. We compressed dozens of substances, chilled them to the limit and let them fuse into a single crystal. It came out beautiful - blue, full of sparks.

The cold off it leaves a burn on the skin, and the living near it seem to forget how to hurry: blood thickens, breath draws out. But the limit we were after we never reached - fell the smallest bit short of true zero. The sample is unstable, and that "smallest bit" gave us pause: on the last test the crystal fell wholly silent, and with it the clock on the wall.

This batch we write off as scrap. But the direction is right - the boys are already preparing the next crystal, and it will come far nearer to absolute zero than ours. If it works, it will overturn everything we knew about superconductors. A pity only that the one who began it won't be the one to test the perfected thing.

GearFeather.name: Gear Feather
GearFeather.story:
First there was the <g>Hopoo Feather</c> - light, rare, almost mythical. Selling it was our first mistake: we lost more than we gained.

Now another feather is in our hands - <g>Gear</c>. Larger, heavier, yet it still pulls you upward when you leap. Outwardly similar, but inside it feels alien. Not a gift, but a bargain.

I wrote in the report: perhaps the first sale was a mistake. I don't want the second mistake to be <y>a dead end at the steam valve</c>.

Artifacts change owners, change worlds. Sometimes they don't break immediately - but they do break nonetheless.

FearEyes.name: Eyes of Fear
FearEyes.pickup: Deals extra damage. Breaks at low health.
FearEyes.description: Increases <y>damage by 15</c><c_stack>(+10 per stack)</c>. Breaks when <r>health drops</c> below <b>40%</c>.
FearEyes.story:
At first, I thought it was just a fish. Yes, fugu - I'd heard of those. But the eyes... they seemed to bulge out and freeze, as if it had seen something better left unseen. I placed it on the table - and it felt like its gaze was still drilling into me.

I don't know if it can be cooked, it's too strange.

I couldn't throw it away. Now it just lies in a container. Staring...

ShadowShield.name: Shadow Shield
ShadowShield.pickup: Do not attack enemies while you are in shadow.
ShadowShield.description: Upon entering a new stage, grants <b>shadow for 30</c><c_stack>(+10 per stack)</c> <b>seconds</c>, increases <b>movement speed by 20%</c><c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c>, and <b>armor by 40</c><c_stack>(+20 per stack)</c>. If the player <y>attacks</c>, the effect dissipates.
ShadowShield.story:
The full moon is the Shield of Faith:
As long as it shall rise,
I know that Mystery comes again,
That Wonder never dies.

I know that Shadow has its place,
That Noon is not our goal,
That Heaven has non-official hours
To soothe and mend the soul;

That witchcraft can be angel-craft
And wizard deeds sublime;
That utmost darkness bears a flower,
Though long the budding-time.

Rhinoplasty.name: Rhinoplasty
Rhinoplasty.description: Killing every <b>3</c> enemies grants <g>1 'skin'</c>, up to <b>3</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c>. After <b>10 seconds</c>, each <g>'piece of skin'</c> is absorbed and restores <g>40</c> <c_stack>(+20 per stack)</c> <g>health</c>.
Rhinoplasty.story:
Attention everyone!
Due to the development of new tools, we need to replace the surgical kits!
Each kit must now include the following: scalpels, clamps, sutures, sterile masks, anesthesia capsules, blood reserves, tissue implant cards, and an auto-injector for coagulation.

And also - a new prototype knife. The blade is made of green medical metal, with a twisted vessel running along it. Upon contact, it takes part of the skin from the wound and returns it to the body. I don't know whether to call this progress, but the scientists insist these tools be kept locked away. Just in case they decide to work without a patient.

GreenTea.name: Green Tea
GreenTea.description: Increases <g>health regeneration</c> by <g>0.546</c> <c_stack>(+0.546 per stack)</c> <g>units</c>. Increases <g>maximum health pool</c> by <g>6.8%</c> <c_stack>(+6.8% per stack)</c>.
GreenTea.destination:
Sheridan S.
Northlake,
UNITED STATES OF AMERICA,
Mars
GreenTea.story:
Mixed up a trial batch: an extract from that giant ginseng you sent for your cancer research, plus a drop of that healing gel from the vial - you know the one, with the whole laundry list of side effects down the label. Brewed some ordinary green tea over the top, for taste. Came out interesting.

Apart, one's a dubious little root and the other's poison with healing thrown in. Together, and watered down at that, they behave themselves: cuts close faster, breathing steadies, strength picks up. No rash, no itch - as if I took half of each and left the worse half in the glass. Worst of all, it tastes good.

That was two weeks ago. I've kept brewing it since. I tell myself it's for the notes. I drink it when there's nothing left to heal. Try it and tell me straight whether it's time to quit - because asking myself no longer works.

Accountant.name: Accountant
Accountant.description: Activating equipment increases <y>attack speed</c> for <y>6 seconds</c> <c_stack>(+3 seconds per stack)</c>. The bonus depends on the equipment's base cooldown, up to <y>60%</c>.
Accountant.story:
A transparent vessel, inside — a brain suspended in viscous fluid. Cables run into the wall, carrying a continuous stream of data. We use it for resource accounting, tax calculations, and supply forecasts.

Once, this was someone's brain. The name was erased, as was the past — only its function remained: to calculate, store, and process. And it does so flawlessly, more precisely than any machine. But sometimes, in rare glitches, words appear on the screen that should not exist: "I'm cold" or "Where are my hands?"

BloodyRelic.name: Bloody Relic
BloodyRelic.description: Dealing damage has a <y>10%</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> chance to grant <r>bloodlust</c>, which increases <y>attack speed</c> by <y>5%</c> for <y>8 seconds</c>. Effect can stack up to <b>5</c> <c_stack>(+1 per stack)</c> <b>times</c>.
BloodyRelic.story:
We thought the pauldron and the skull were the limit of what twisted rebel minds could create. But under a pile of rusted blades, we found a necklace. The stone in the center looks like an eye, and it's <r>red</c>, as if filled with fresh blood. It stares straight at you, even when you look away.

Rumors spread quickly among the workers: the relic gives "thirst" - the more blood you spill, the faster you move, the more eagerly your hands raise weapons. One guard held it, and now he utters no sound, his gaze darting as though he hears the heartbeat of those who can still be killed. They shackled him and took him to the med bay.

In short - don't remove it from the container, don't let anyone touch it without proper gear, etc., etc., standard safety. I don't need to explain that to you.

GoldBar.name: Gold Bar
GoldBar.pickup: Grants <y>gold</c> at the start of a stage.
GoldBar.description: Upon entering a stage grants <y>$33</c> <c_stack>(+22 per stack)</c>. <y>Increases over time</c>.
GoldBar.story:
Pulled from an old safe during the dig at the estate of some long-vanished rich men. To look at it, an ordinary gold bar. But marks are etched along its edge, like figures. The jewelers agreed to a man: it's no stamp and no hallmark - the metal seems to have always borne them.

The figures won't hold still. The cashier who held the bar swore the number crept slowly upward, right there in his hand, and the longer you held it the heavier it pressed on your palm. He set it down when he understood - that isn't a price climbing. That's a debt.

Take it into safekeeping. Paper burns, accounts reset to zero, but gold stays gold. I only rest easier when this bar is lying in someone else's hands.

GiftPurity.name: Gift of Purity
GiftPurity.pickup: <b>The third skill recharges faster</c> and <g>grants barrier</c>
GiftPurity.description: <b>Reduces cooldown</c> of the third skill by <b>15%</c><c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c>. Using the <b>third skill</c> grants a temporary <g>barrier</c> for <g>1.5%</c><c_stack>(+0.5% per stack)</c> of your <g>maximum health</c> per second of the skill's base cooldown.
GiftPurity.destination:
Sanctuary of the Seer,
Hall Beneath the Moonlit Dome,
[REDACTED]
GiftPurity.story:
The Gift of Purity is the rarest sign of mercy. Those who receive it find in their hands a pair of smooth, <y>violet</c> berries, exuding a faint scent of the night air. They are not harvested, not cultivated, not passed from hand to hand. It is believed they can only be received by the will of the Seer - and only by those who have proven their devotion.

Unlike the beads that bring anxiety and death, the Gift of Purity brings silence and confidence. Those fortunate enough to carry it said that enemies seemed to hesitate, blows lost their sharpness, and the heart grew calm. No voices, no whispers, no laughter. Only the sense that an unseen hand stands nearby, and your life - is no longer your own.

Yet this is no simple gift. The berries are a symbol. While they are fresh, you are under protection, but betrayal turns them into poison. Stories tell of deserters who found only shriveled fruits in their palms, bitter and deadly to the taste. The Seer, it is said, does not forgive those who break their oath.

Still, for communities that endured trials, the appearance of the Gift is a blessing. It reminds: the Seer not only punishes, but also protects. Where beads carry terror and dread, the berries of purity bring hope and peace. Their rarity makes them elusive, but all who have heard of them know: it is a promise. A promise that loyalty will be noticed, and the faithful - never forgotten.

GravityLoop.name: Gravity Loop
GravityLoop.description: When <r>health drops</c> below <b>35%</c>, <g>restores 70% health</c>. Breaks after use.
GravityLoop.destination:
Biocollection No.3,
Greenhouse and Aquarium,
New-Gravenkamp, Earth
GravityLoop.story:
Good day.
I dug this trinket up at a mountain excavation. It lay beside a skeleton that a collapse like that should have ground to dust - yet the bones were whole, not a crack on them. The device, by contrast, is all cracks, fit to crumble at a breath.

Through my own carelessness I tripped and dropped it onto the rocks. I thought the find was done for. But neither it nor I took any harm: it was as though it drank the whole blow itself. Once.

Later I looked up the histories of its former owners. Every one was found like that first skeleton: not a scratch on the body, beside them the same dead box, burned out from within. Each survived the thing that should have killed him - and soon died of another: one finished off by hunger, one by the cold. I'm sending it all the same: beyond its age, the personal-protection market will pay well for this. Only warn the buyer honestly - it will save you once, and no more.

Obereg.name: Obereg
Obereg.pickup: The lower your health, the greater your survivability.
Obereg.description: When <r>health</c> falls below <r>50%</c>, increases <y>armor</c> by <b>20</c> <c_stack>(+20 per stack)</c>.
Obereg.destination:
Old mound near the Black Forest,
A village erased from the map,
[REDACTED]
Obereg.story:
When the enemy warbands came, the people had neither walls nor an army. An old woman brought forth a green star, forged from strange metal, from her oven and placed it on the threshold. "Let the house at least stand, while we yet live," she said.

The warriors fell one by one, and only when the snow was soaked with blood did the amulet begin to glow. It held their souls to their bodies, forcing them to rise so long as hands could still grasp an axe. But its light was cold and cruel: the brighter it burned, the faster life slipped away. Since then, it was said - this metal does not forgive the weak, it only delays death.

When the mound was covered in grass, only the star remained. Heavy, cold, with a rusty glint of alien blood.

ShieldGenerator.name: Shield Generator
ShieldGenerator.description: Your <b>drones</c> gain a shield equal to <b>10%</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> of their <b>maximum health</c>.
ShieldGenerator.destination:
Energy Bay,
Field Testing Department,
Station "Asterion"
ShieldGenerator.story:
Item: wearable shield-field generator. Housing - a cast block, an active core.

At rest - a steady blue glow, a faint hiss. Under load the sensors register a sharp spike in the field. Mounting is standard, onto armor.

The field absorbs an incoming blow in part; the remainder falls on the wearer. Beneath the field one hears one's own sounds returned - an incidental acoustic effect, no bearing on function.

Noted over the trial period: wearers under the field more often take unjustified risks. No restrictions on issue.

EyeDiamond.name: Diamond Eye
EyeDiamond.description: Increases <y>critical hit chance</c> by <y>10%</c> and <r>critical hit damage</c> by <r>150%</c> <c_stack>(+100% per stack)</c>.
EyeDiamond.destination:
Gravenkamp,
5th Houston Street,
Prism Tower,
Earth
EyeDiamond.story:
Your order has been processed. The frame is made of synthetic diamond, the lenses tinted <r>red</c>. The right lens has an integrated targeting module - it activates automatically, creating a glow visible even through fog.

I must warn you: these materials are far too expensive for a mere accessory. Considering your past orders, one gets the impression you're not looking for optics, but for a weapon. Please - if you are working for [REDACTED], be cautious. These glasses were made to <r>cut</c> more precisely, not to see further.

AtGMissileMk0.name: Air-to-Ground Missile Mk. 0
AtGMissileMk0.description: On hit, you have a <y>7% chance</c> <c_stack>(+7% per stack)</c> to launch a missile dealing <y>75% TOTAL damage</c>.
AtGMissileMk0.destination:
Fort Joseph,
Belt No.1053,
[REDACTED],
Boron System
AtGMissileMk0.story:
Delivery of the prototype Viper Missile System.

Single-barrel configuration, extremely unreliable ignition system. Missiles often misfire or trigger prematurely, yet command insisted on field testing. Ammunition limited to a single launch, recommended for training purposes only.

Do not issue to regular units. According to engineers, the launcher poses a greater threat to the operator than to the target.

SporeOBloom.name: Sporebloom
SporeOBloom.description:
On killing an enemy, has a 44% chance to release a

poison cloud dealing 60% (+60% per stack) damage over 5 seconds.
SporeOBloom.destination:
Hidden Nook,
Mountain of Creation,
Venus
SporeOBloom.story:
A funny thing happened once - in a rush I forgot a piece of bread inside my bag. Later, when I noticed a strange smell and opened it, the bread was already covered in moss, with a tiny purple mushroom growing nearby.
I carefully placed it in a jar with moist moss. Mushrooms, as I know, feed on moisture and decaying matter. So I spray it with water and give it small pieces of bread. It doesn't need green light or fertilizer, it just draws in dampness and absorbs the bread, releasing spores in the process. It even reacts to words - if you whisper something, it slightly raises its cap, as if listening. The main thing is not to let the jar dry out, and make sure the moss stays damp.
I'm sending it to you - best not to unscrew the lid indoors or place it near a fan. Once, after a draft, spores swirled everywhere inside and it hid in the moss for half a day. It seems to prefer twilight and being spoken to in a soft voice.

NewtonsMechanism.name: Newton's Mechanism
NewtonsMechanism.pickup: <b>Electricity</c> is not a toy for children.
NewtonsMechanism.description: While you have a shield, grants <y>+5 armor</c> <c_stack>(+5 per stack)</c>. When the <b>shield breaks</c>, releases a <y>stunning pulse</c> for <y>150%</c> <c_stack>(+125% per stack)</c> <y>base damage</c>, plus <y>125%</c> <c_stack>(+100% per stack)</c> of <b>maximum shield</c>.
NewtonsMechanism.story: Hey, sending you this exhibit for your Newton memorial museum. I think it would fit Mr. Newton himself perfectly, heh! Just make sure when unpacking it you handle it with the utmost care, and once you place it in the museum, keep it in an impact-resistant container filled with that cushioning fluid we talked about earlier. Still, I advise keeping it at least 2 meters away - you wouldn't want your visitors turning into toasted pancakes, right?

CoolGlasses.name: Cool Glasses
CoolGlasses.description: Increases <y>critical hit chance by 5%</c>. On a <y>critical hit</c> creates a <r>solar strike</c>, dealing <y>20%</c> <c_stack>(+20% per stack)</c> <y>TOTAL damage</c> in a <b>2m</c> radius.
CoolGlasses.destination:
Gravenkamp,
5th Houston Street,
Prism Tower,
Earth
CoolGlasses.story:
A simple frame, dark lenses, nothing remarkable at first glance. But under stress they overheat and cast a glow like a little <y>sun</c>. With your hands, the effect becomes even more dangerous.

I do not understand why you need two different sets at once. You asked for diamond lenses, now a black sun. If these are all parts of one project, you are playing with something that could easily get out of control.

DeepcoreGK2.name: Deepcore GK2
DeepcoreGK2.description: Grants <b>5% shield</c> for each drone. Gives <y>'Bosco'</c>, which periodically fires at enemies, dealing <y>5% damage</c> and <b>slowing</c> them. Number of targets: <y>2</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c>, target range: <b>10m</c> <c_stack>(+1m per stack)</c>. Bosco's fire rate depends on <y>attack speed</c>.
DeepcoreGK2.story: Yo, thanks for the drone parts - they fit my boys like a glove. In return I'm sending you a pretty rare piece - not exactly contraband, but getting it was a real pain. A GK-series assault rifle and a (slightly worn) Autonomous Support Drone (A.S.D.) - exclusive gear from the military-mining company in the Hoxxes system! You'll figure out the rifle yourself, you're no fool, but about the drone - damn, even worn-out, I'd have kept it for myself. Most of its functions are gone, but it still has its laser! Can't say for sure, but it's a patented technology that allows it to remain functional almost indefinitely without losing combat potential. Just make sure no one finds out you've got it - the dwarf who sold it to me went through hell to get it.

Totemetry.name: Totemetry
Totemetry.pickup: Periodically creates a totem that increases <y>attack speed</c> and <g>heals</c> in a large radius.
Totemetry.description: Every <b>2 minutes</c> creates a totem lasting <b>45 seconds</c>, which <g>heals allies</c> in a <y>7.5m</c> radius <c_stack>(+2m per stack)</c> for <g>2%</c> <c_stack>(+2% per stack)</c> of their <g>maximum health</c>. The appearance of the totem increases <y>attack speed</c> by <y>10%</c>.
Totemetry.destination:
Excavations in the Fungal Gorge,
Observation Sector,
[REDACTED]
Totemetry.story:
We found it among the debris - a green rectangular stone, as if carved by hands that never existed here. At the first touch it trembled, and nearby a <g>totem</c> appeared, resembling the structures raised by lynxes. It glowed with a soft light, and everyone within range felt lighter, their breathing steadier.

It seems this artifact is not merely an ornament, but a portable shrine. The lynx tribe uses it to gather together: it quickens their rhythm, lifts their spirit, heals their wounds. Stranger still - the stone activates on its own, as though deciding itself when its bearer needs to recover.

In it echo the songs of the lynx, their laughter and their dances. As if each activation brings back a piece of the tribe, even if they have long since returned to the earth. I do not know whose voice will answer the next time it shines.

RadialAlloy.name: Radial Alloy
RadialAlloy.description: After <r>taking damage</c>, <g>heals</c> for <g>12</c> <c_stack>(+12 per stack)</c> <g>HP</c>. <g>Healing</c> can offset incoming <r>damage</c> no lower than <b>5 HP</c>.
RadialAlloy.destination:
Materials Science Laboratory,
Research Sector No.12,
Mars
RadialAlloy.story:
Shards of metal, cut as if at a slant - faceted, as though by a beam. They tremble faintly in the hand.

Noted: if a wounded man holds them, the wound closes noticeably faster, as though the metal shares its strength with the body. By structure - an ordinary crystalline lattice, nothing special. By behavior - something else.

It doesn't take the pain away. It takes the pain for itself. Whoever carried a handful of the shards too long began complaining of wounds not his own: an aching break he never suffered, a burn from a fire long gone cold. In the report we put it briefly - "material with a healing effect." Among ourselves we argue about something else: where does all it has gathered go, once there is no room left inside it?

Maslenitsa.name: Maslenitsa
Maslenitsa.pickup: Increases <g>armor</c>. Grants <g>armor</c> for items that provide <g>armor</c>.
Maslenitsa.description: Increases <g>armor by 15</c> <c_stack>(+15 per stack)</c>. Additionally grants <g>2</c> <c_stack>(+3 per stack)</c> <g>armor</c> for each of: 'Hard Times', 'Reflective Armor', 'Huge Cone', 'Obereg', 'Shadow Shield', 'Faceted Jade', 'Newton's Mechanism'.
Maslenitsa.destination:
Hill with a burned village,
Abandoned shrine,
[REDACTED]
Maslenitsa.story:
When winter dragged on for a third year, people gathered on the hill and lit a great fire. Not for celebration - but for the final rite. They placed a green helmet, hammered with the sun itself, upon the head of the strongest warrior.

He stood, heavy as the earth itself, and the enemies could not break his armor. But each strike tore breath from his chest, and the helmet pressed him ever closer to the ground. People cried, sang, and threw pancakes into the fire, hoping to appease the sun. But spring never came.

The helmet was returned to the shrine after the warrior fell and never rose again. Now it lies in silence, the metal still bearing the gleam of those fires that warmed no one.

ShineOSun.name: Shine, O Sun
ShineOSun.pickup: Chance to create a <y>sun</c> when killing enemies.
ShineOSun.description: On killing an enemy, has a <y>22%</c> chance to create a <y>scorching sun</c>, dealing <r>5% of the enemy's maximum health</c> within a <b>12m</c> radius <c_stack>(+2.5m per stack)</c> for 5 seconds.
ShineOSun.destination:
Hidden Nook,
Mountain of Creation,
Venus
ShineOSun.story:
Guys, remember the little mushroom I sent yesterday? Today it has a new neighbor in the jar.
A tiny sun with little hands. I'm not joking — it is warm like a cup of tea. When I made it laugh, its temperature rose and it melted the label on the jar. It does not ask for food either; as I noticed, it feeds on sunlight (though it is itself like a little sun). Caution: do not place it near heaters or shake it — the glass clicked a couple of times from the heat.
If it is awake, its light can illuminate the apartment at night, so keep it under a blanket or in a darkened box. In the evening I had to wrap the jar in a towel. Sometimes I tapped the glass, and it began to move and peek out as if from behind a curtain. I am sending it to you — I hope you have a nanny lamp so it will not be lonely.

Dombra.name: Dombra
Dombra.pickup: Chance to summon <r>eagle wings</c> on hit.
Dombra.description: On hit, you have a <y>20% chance</c> to summon <r>berkut wings</c> to your <b>left and right</c>, together dealing <y>122% TOTAL damage</c> <c_stack>(+122% per stack)</c>.
Dombra.story:
A kuy needs no words.

So said the man who owned this dombra. When asked how two strings could tell of home, the road, death, victory, and return, he only smiled and placed his fingers on the wood.

The first sound was dry as grass beneath the sun. The second was low as distant thunder. With the third, the sound went farther than it should have, rolling through the stone, metal, bones, and weapons of everyone standing nearby.

On the fourth...

On the fifth, the sky answered.

VioletFrenzy.name: Violet Frenzy
VioletFrenzy.pickup:
Killing an elite sends you into a

frenzy with near-instant cooldowns.
VioletFrenzy.description:
Killing an elite monster sends you into a

berserker state for 8 seconds (+6 seconds per stack). Berserker increases movement speed by 30%, and using a skill reduces the cooldown of other skills by 50%.
VioletFrenzy.story:
The container holds residual materials from Mr. Jefferson's samples. The primary brain was sent intact, as separating the glowing stalks without damaging the tissue was impossible. An anomaly was discovered during a second inspection: the stalks react to electrical stimulation.

The glow changed from pale blue to violet. Signals through the damaged brain accelerated to a level not classified as human. For 47 seconds, the dead tissue showed activity corresponding to panic, pain, delight, and aggression. After that, the stalks crumbled into glowing dust.

TwistedOpinion.name: Twisted Opinion
TwistedOpinion.pickup:
Healing is amplified and stored as

soul energy that strikes with skulls.
TwistedOpinion.description:
Healing is increased by 75% (+50% per stack). 100% (+75% per stack) of healing is stored as

soul energy. When

soul energy reaches 10% of your maximum health, a skull is released, dealing 150% of the stored

soul energy.
TwistedOpinion.destination:
Abandoned graveyard,
The well by the three aspens,
[REDACTED]
TwistedOpinion.story:
"Nature nourishes the body. Resolve nourishes the mind. Imagination nourishes the soul." The later disciples of N'kuhana found this line incomplete.

They added a fourth principle: death feeds on excess.

If death is the great equalizer, then healing is a debt taken from it without permission. The resulting surplus cannot be considered a blessing.

Not every debt should be repaid at once. Some debts must be grown. Strengthened. Multiplied. Until life itself becomes heavy enough for matter to be chipped from it.

Technician.name: Technician
Technician.description: More drones appear in the stage. The more players there are, the more drones appear.

Droneman.name: Droneman
Droneman.description: When survivors become drones, they inherits their items

Victory.name: Victory
Victory.description: Defeating the teleporter boss revives fallen players at full health.

Stagnation.name: Stagnation
Stagnation.description: The difficulty timer is disabled. During a teleporter event, the timer grows x5 faster.

Storm.name: Storm
Storm.description: Lightning strikes near the players. Lightning strikes take away 5% of your maximum health. Items enhance the storm.
